input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-03T01:17:51.684Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically. When the user toggles the OS theme, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates live — no restart needed.

---

**C# equivalent — `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
BackgroundColor = new Color(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF); // initial
this.SetAppThemeColor(
    BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E")); // dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Colors.White);

// For non-color properties, use the generic version:
label.SetAppTheme<string>(
    Label.TextProperty,
    "Light Mode",
    "Dark Mode");
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `OSAppTheme` from device settings |
| **Live update** | Yes — reacts at runtime, no reload |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force a theme |
| **Fallback** | If `Unspecified`, the `Light` value is used |

Replace your hardcoded colors with `AppThemeBinding` pairs and the page becomes fully theme-aware with zero code-behind required.
